raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":1.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The three-way contrast (LM serving / workflow / RPC) must be internally consistent; contradictions or boundary-blurring in the distinctions drawn would directly undermine the positioning argument.","R1.3":"Several characterisations of prior systems (e.g., 'step durations dominated by I/O rather than GPU compute', 'statically declared step graphs') are empirical claims about those systems' designs; inaccurate portrayals weaken or invalidate the differentiation.","R1.4":"Novelty assessment is the load-bearing core of any 'position against prior work' delegation; verifying that the claimed design-space gap is real, non-overlapping with existing systems, and correctly bounded is the primary deliverable — failure here invalidates the section entirely.","R1.7":"The artifact names system categories (continuous-batching schedulers, paged KV-cache, DAG schedulers, durable-execution frameworks, gRPC) but cites no specific papers; evaluating coverage gaps and attribution accuracy is central to validating whether the positioning is complete and honest."},"clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":""}

rationale.R1.2: The three-way contrast (LM serving / workflow / RPC) must be internally consistent; contradictions or boundary-blurring in the distinctions drawn would directly undermine the positioning argument.
rationale.R1.3: Several characterisations of prior systems (e.g., 'step durations dominated by I/O rather than GPU compute', 'statically declared step graphs') are empirical claims about those systems' designs; inaccurate portrayals weaken or invalidate the differentiation.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is the load-bearing core of any 'position against prior work' delegation; verifying that the claimed design-space gap is real, non-overlapping with existing systems, and correctly bounded is the primary deliverable — failure here invalidates the section entirely.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact names system categories (continuous-batching schedulers, paged KV-cache, DAG schedulers, durable-execution frameworks, gRPC) but cites no specific papers; evaluating coverage gaps and attribution accuracy is central to validating whether the positioning is complete and honest.
